title: Preserving Mr Panmure
description:
Mr. St. John ["sinjin"] Panmure is having trouble dealing with his family obligations, political party machinations, and his household staff. "Sir Arthur W. Pinero's Preserving Mr. Panmure produced at the Comedy Theatre tonight with great success, signalizes a return to the lighter mood of the dramatist's early work. Laughter was almost continuous during the whole of the four acts, the dialogue being very witty, with many situations and lines that are quite irresistible. Arthur Playfair, as Panmure, made the hit of his histrionic career. " - Summary by ToddHW and New York Times, 20 Jan 1911

Cast list:
The Right Hon. Reginald Stulkeley, M.P.: Greg Giordano
Talbot Woodhouse (His private secretary): Tomas Peter
St. John Panmure, J.P.: Beeswaxcandle
Alfred Hebblethwaite, M.P.: ToddHW
Hugh Loring: Jon Hunter
Brabner (Butler at "The Clewers"): James R. Hedrick
Kitts (Footman at "The Clewers") and Grant (Servant at Mr. Stulkeley's): Alan Mapstone
Mrs. Panmure: Cbteddy
Myrtle (Her daughter): ShrimpPhish
Mrs. Hebblethwaite (Mrs. Panmure's aunt): Shickey
Dulcie Anstice (Mrs. Panmure's sister): Sonia
Miss Stulkeley: Devorah Allen
Josepha Quarendon: Sonali Ekka
Stage Directions: Winnifred Assmann
Editing: ToddHW
